data.title: STOCKS | Surge in Foreign Investment in Japanese Stocks Following Election
data.description: Foreign investors have significantly increased their purchases of Japanese stocks and futures, marking the highest level in over a decade. Bloomberg posted on X that this surge in investment activity occurred in the days following Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's recent election victory. The influx of foreign capital highlights renewed confidence in Japan's economic prospects under Takaichi's leadership. This development is seen as a positive indicator for the country's financial markets, reflecting optimism about potential policy changes and economic growth. The election outcome has evidently influenced investor sentiment, leading to heightened activity in the stock market.

data.title: Indonesia Maintains Key Interest Rate at 4.75%
data.description: Indonesia has decided to keep its benchmark interest rate steady at 4.75%, aligning with market expectations. According to RTHK, this marks the fifth consecutive time the rate has remained unchanged. The overnight deposit and lending rates are also maintained at 3.75% and 5.5%, respectively. The central bank emphasized its current focus on stabilizing the exchange rate while continuing to assess the economic impact of previous rate cuts. Between September 2024 and the previous year, Indonesia cumulatively reduced its benchmark rate by 1.5%. Recently, the Indonesian rupiah depreciated against the U.S. dollar, nearing a record low.

data.title: Solana Advisor Criticizes Prediction Market Spam on X Platform
data.description: Nikita Bier, the product lead for X and an advisor for Solana, has expressed concerns over the excessive spam related to prediction markets on the X platform. According to Odaily, Bier highlighted the issue by sharing a scenic image, captioned to illustrate what the X platform would look like without the clutter of prediction market spam.

data.title: Anglo American's Iron Ore Unit Surpasses Profit Expectations
data.description:
Anglo American's South African iron ore division has reported a full-year profit that exceeded market forecasts. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the unit's strong performance amid challenging market conditions. The company attributed its success to efficient operations and favorable commodity prices, which contributed to higher-than-expected earnings.

The iron ore unit's robust results come as a positive development for Anglo American, which has been navigating fluctuating demand and pricing in the global iron ore market. Analysts had anticipated a solid performance, but the actual figures surpassed those predictions, underscoring the division's resilience and strategic management.

This achievement reflects the broader trends in the mining sector, where companies are adapting to volatile market dynamics and optimizing their operations to maintain profitability. Anglo American's focus on cost management and operational efficiency has played a crucial role in achieving these results.

The company's leadership expressed satisfaction with the outcome, noting that the iron ore unit's performance is a testament to the hard work and dedication of its workforce. Looking ahead, Anglo American plans to continue leveraging its strengths to capitalize on market opportunities and sustain its growth trajectory.

data.title: X Product Manager Nikita Bier criticized the platform for having too much spam in its prediction markets.
data.description: Nikita Bier, X's product lead and Solana consultant, criticized the X platform for excessive prediction market spam. He posted a picture of a beautiful environment with the caption, "This is the X platform without prediction market spam."

data.title: Uxlink Hacker Resurfaces, Acquires ETH with DAI
data.description: The Uxlink hacker has become active again after a three-month hiatus, purchasing Ethereum (ETH) using DAI. Analyst @ai_9684xtpa posted on X that four addresses associated with the hacker acquired 5,493.26 ETH, valued at $10.87 million, with an average cost of $1,979.27 per ETH, just an hour ago. The hacker still holds 21.42 million DAI, indicating potential further purchases.

data.title: The UXLINK hacker bought 5,493 ETH after three months and still holds 21.42 million DAI.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, the UXLINK hacker (uxlink-exploiter) has become active again after a three-month hiatus. One hour ago, the hacker's four addresses spent $10.87 million in DAI to buy 5493.26 ETH, at an average purchase price of $1979.27. The hacker currently holds 21.42 million DAI and may continue to buy.

data.title: Mining companies linked to the UAE have accumulated $344 million in unrealized profits.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, Citadel Mining, an operation linked to the Abu Dhabi royal family, currently holds $453.6 million worth of Bitcoin, generating $344 million in unrealized profits excluding energy costs. Data shows that the country has maintained a daily production rate of 4.2 BTC over the past seven days through mining via its state-linked infrastructure. The UAE currently holds approximately 6,782 BTC, representing 0.03% of the total Bitcoin supply. On-chain records show that the country retains the majority of its self-mined Bitcoin, with the most recent outflow occurring four months ago. Furthermore, Arkham compared this to other government holdings: the Bhutanese government currently holds 5,600 BTC, worth $375 million; the US government holds 328,000 BTC, worth $22 billion; and the UK government holds 61,000 BTC, worth $4 billion.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Silver Stocks Strengthen in Pre-Market Trading
data.description: Silver-related stocks showed strength in pre-market trading. According to Jin10, Pan American Silver (PAAS.O) rose by 4.6%, Hecla Mining (HL.N) increased by 1.9%, and First Majestic (AG.O) gained 1.3%.

data.title: Technology's Benefits Highlighted by Alphabet CEO Amid Concerns Over AI Divide
data.description: Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai emphasized the significant advantages technology offers, while cautioning against the potential for a digital divide to evolve into an AI divide. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Pichai's remarks during India's AI summit. Pichai underscored the importance of ensuring equitable access to AI technologies to prevent disparities in technological benefits. He stressed that while AI has the potential to transform industries and improve lives, it is crucial to address accessibility issues to avoid widening existing gaps. The summit serves as a platform for discussing the future of AI and its implications for global communities.

data.title: TECHNOLOGY | Software Companies Face Challenges Amid 'SaaSpocalypse'
data.description:
The software industry is experiencing significant upheaval, with many companies facing challenges in the current market environment. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the difficulties that software-as-a-service (SaaS) companies are encountering as they navigate economic uncertainties and shifting consumer demands.

Industry experts suggest that only those companies with strong financial foundations and innovative offerings are likely to endure the so-called 'SaaSpocalypse.' The term refers to the intense competition and market saturation affecting SaaS providers, leading to increased pressure on pricing and profitability.

As businesses reassess their software needs, companies that can adapt quickly and offer unique solutions are expected to thrive. Analysts emphasize the importance of strategic planning and customer-centric approaches in ensuring long-term success.

The current landscape presents both challenges and opportunities for software companies, with those able to leverage technology and maintain customer loyalty standing a better chance of survival. The focus remains on innovation and efficiency as key drivers for overcoming the hurdles posed by the 'SaaSpocalypse.'

data.title: Arab Bank's Fintech Accelerator Invests in Stablecoin Settlement Firm Ubyx
data.description: Arab Bank's fintech accelerator, AB Xelerate, has announced an investment in stablecoin settlement company Ubyx, according to PANews. The specific amount of the investment has not been disclosed. The new funding will support the development of a shared network that enables regulated financial institutions to issue, accept, and exchange digital currencies at face value across multiple blockchains and jurisdictions, while operating within established regulatory and compliance frameworks. Previously, Ubyx was acquired by banking giant Barclays, which plans to explore the development of 'tokenized currency.'

data.title: Banks and Crypto Executives Set for White House Stablecoin Summit
data.description:
Key Takeaways: A third White House meeting on stablecoin yield is scheduled for 9:00 a. m. ET.
source: https://coindoo.com/banks-and-crypto-executives-set-for-white-house-stablecoin-summit/

data.title: Bitcoin ETFs see $133M outflows as sentiment stays in ‘extreme fear’
data.description:
US-listed spot Bitcoin ETFs have shed $238 million this week, setting up the first five-week outflow streak since March 2025.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/bitcoin-etf-133-million-outflows-extreme-fear-sentiment?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Ethereum Protocol Roadmap 2026: Scaling, Account Abstraction and Quantum Readiness Enter Core Phase
data.description:
Ethereum core developers unveiled 2026 protocol priorities centered on scaling, native account abstraction and quantum-resistant security ahead of the Glamsterdam upgrade.

The post Ethereum Protocol Roadmap 2026: Scaling, Account Abstraction and Quantum Readiness Enter Core Phase first appeared on Crypto News And Market Updates | BTCUSA.

source: https://btcusa.com/ethereum-protocol-roadmap-2026-scaling-account-abstraction-and-quantum-readiness-enter-core-phase/

data.title: HyperLiquid will hold a validator vote on February 23 for the removal of OM.
data.description: HyperLiquid announced on its X platform that validators will vote around 8:00 UTC on February 23 (16:00 Beijing time) to decide whether to delist OM. If the validators vote to delist OM, traders should settle their positions to the 1-hour time-weighted spot oracle price before delisting and cancel all open orders. Therefore, it is necessary to close positions in advance to avoid automatic settlement. After settlement, no new orders will be accepted.

data.title: France's Budget Deficit Reduction Strategy Faces Uncertainty
data.description: France's efforts to decrease its budget deficit this year are facing significant uncertainty, according to the country's audit court. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges in achieving fiscal targets amid economic pressures. The audit court's report underscores the difficulties in implementing effective measures to curb the deficit, which has been a persistent issue for the French government. The court's assessment raises concerns about the feasibility of the current strategy and suggests that more robust actions may be necessary to address the fiscal imbalance. The report comes at a time when France is grappling with broader economic challenges, including inflation and slow growth, which complicate efforts to stabilize public finances. The audit court's findings emphasize the need for careful planning and execution to ensure that deficit reduction goals are met.

data.title: OpenEden Restores DNS Control After Unauthorized Domain Hijacking
data.description: OpenEden, a platform for tokenizing real-world assets, announced on X that it has regained full control over its website domain and subdomains' DNS settings, restoring normal operations. According to ChainCatcher, OpenEden revealed that its website experienced unauthorized DNS record modifications, leading to a domain hijacking incident. Following a joint investigation with security partners and infrastructure service providers, it was confirmed that the event did not affect any smart contracts, internal systems were not breached, core applications remained intact, and reserve assets were unaffected. All reserve assets can be independently verified through Chainlink's Proof of Reserve. OpenEden plans to enhance security measures for its domain and infrastructure.

data.title: Swiss National Bank Develops Standardized Process for ELF Project
data.description: The Swiss National Bank (SNB) has announced the establishment of a standardized and scalable process for the 'ELF' project. According to Jin10, this initiative will enable participating banks to swiftly access liquidity support backed by collateral when necessary. The move is aimed at enhancing the financial stability and operational efficiency of banks involved in the project. The SNB's approach underscores its commitment to providing robust financial infrastructure and support mechanisms in times of need.

data.title: Pershing Square Capital Shifts Focus to Tech Stocks with Meta Investment
data.description: Billionaire investor Bill Ackman's Pershing Square Capital Management made a significant investment in Meta Platforms, acquiring $1.76 billion worth of shares in the fourth quarter of 2025. According to NS3.AI, this move coincided with the firm's complete exit from its stake in Chipotle Mexican Grill. Additionally, Pershing Square reduced its holdings in Alphabet, Uber, and Restaurant Brands International during the same period. These strategic adjustments indicate a shift in the firm's portfolio towards tech stocks, particularly Meta and Alphabet, despite the mixed performance trends in the sector.
